title: Ability to "select all boards" within dashboard widgets
excerpt: Our team uses over 40 boards. I’m excited about the new ‘workload’ widget, but need all of my team’s work visible to make it accurate & useful. Currently we are limited to selecting 10 boards, so the information is only …

posts.author: eglasier (Erica Glasier)
posts.cookedHtml:
Our team uses over 40 boards. I’m excited about the new ‘workload’ widget, but need all of my team’s work visible to make it accurate & useful. Currently we are limited to selecting 10 boards, so the information is only 1/4 of my team’s actual workloads.

Unless there is a way to select all your boards and I just can’t find it…

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @eglasier
Welcome to the community and thanks for reaching out!

I’ve sent you a message in private about this
Cheers,
Julia

posts.author: rbinstock (Randi Binstock)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Julia, I am looking to do the same in terms of selecting all boards to a workload widget. Can you let me know how this can be done?

posts.author: KevinAtomic (Kevin Willingham)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello @Julia-monday.com I’d love to speak to someone about this as well. We’re a smaller company so Enterprise doesn’t seem to be the right fit but we’re already over that 25 board limit anyway. We would love to be able to have more boards talk to each other in the dashboard for capacity and scheduling management.

posts.author: brooks (Brooks Williams)
posts.cookedHtml:
This would be a nice feature to have.

Or to be able to select all of the boards within a folder - that would work too

posts.author: Concept (Alex)
posts.cookedHtml: True, we also asked about this. There is no point in the dashboard with the restrictions.

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml:
What is the reason to limit this on 10 boards. Is this for performance issues? Is this to try to get companies to enterprise?
For me it is a bit annoying not being able to use the battery and the resource planner to its full potential since we work on more then ten boards. Weird.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Eltjo:

Weird.

Agreed. I can’t think of any other similar software which imposes such arbitrary limits when most of us want to see data for all boards, account-wide.

posts.author: Nemeo (Benoit Richert)
posts.cookedHtml:
Same for us: we are a 10 people company and just switched to Monday, and we have already a bit more than 10 task boards.

Some rare widgets have board blacklist (my week), some have whitelists. Why not normalize this board choosing, and get rid of the limit? Is it a performance thing?

By the way, I asked the price of the entreprise price and it’s unthinkable for a company below 50 people.

posts.author: Bry_ProjectKickstart (Bryan)
posts.cookedHtml: Please message us as well, @Julia-monday.com. I need this for my team and my clients.

posts.author: yasminmfn (Yasmin Nobbs)
posts.cookedHtml: @Julia-monday.com Hi Julia is there anything planned for this one. Definitely a key enhancement for us!

posts.author: mattleno (Gedalia Leonorovitz)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Julia-monday.com
I am looking to do the same in terms of selecting all boards to a workload widget. Can you let me know how this can be done?

posts.author: Karrisa (Karrisa)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Julia-monday.com, our team also just made the move to Monday in hopes that it covered our resourcing needs as well. I’m only just discovering the limitation to 10 boards this morning which won’t work for us considering we’ll probably have 30ish boards/projects at a time.

I also found it frustrating to not be able to filter by team, but we can definitely work around that for now. Are there any plans to remove this 10 board limitation any time soon?

posts.author: rujs (Sjur Brekke)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Julia-monday.com,

Please message me as well.

Also, I wonder why you do not just answer here so everyone can see ?
To be honest I am rather disappointed with the level of feedback you (monday) are providing and it makes me wonder if we should look elsewhere for a project mgmt-tool. We love monday and use it for all of our projects, but we need a tool that can handle way more than e.g 10 boards in dashboards in order to e.g manage resources.

Looking forward to your response

Best regards,

posts.author: BrittanyGo (Brittany O)
posts.cookedHtml: @Julia-monday.com My team desperately needs to be able to see ALL our 30+ boards in the calendar view so we can see an overview of everyone’s tasks for all projects. Please advise on how this can be done. I pushed my team to go to Monday and they’re not happy with it because it is missing this one critical feature - the 10 board limit is incredibly limiting

posts.author: Bry_ProjectKickstart (Bryan)
posts.cookedHtml: @Julia-monday.com - me too on this one. Please let me know how to use dashboard widgets for more than 10 boards. It’s a remarkably and paralyzingly low limit. Anybody who uses a board to manage a client and has more than 10 clients suddenly has zero utility from the dashboards. (That’s us.)

posts.author: Bry_ProjectKickstart (Bryan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Oh. That’s discouraging. I see I also tagged @Julia-monday.com on May 1st to get help and I don’t think I’ve heard back.

For the record, reporting problems because of this limitation just lost me a client and cost me $2,500. So, yeah, it’s a big deal.

(If you are interested in the details, they have 30-50 clients at any given time and they needed aggregated reporting. I spent countless hours trying to solve the problem and thus provided an extremely delayed response on deliverables - trying to find workarounds - and it cost me the relationship). My fault in not managing expectations properly. But, the dozens and dozen of wasted hours trying to find workarounds were debilitating (also my fault). However, it would have been worlds easier to keep that client (and get others like them) if this had been available.

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey guys,

I apologize for the delay in getting back to you all. I’ve asked @Danielle-monday.com to respond here as she is currently the CX who is on this domain and can give a better explanation on why it is currently limited and what’s planned next.

Thanks for your patience!

posts.author: Danielle-monday.com (Danielle Glickman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everybody!

We are aware of the need to increase the amount of boards on the dashboards and are working to allow this soon! I cannot provide an ETA at the moment but know that we hear you and are planning on increasing this as soon as possible!

While we wish we could grant unlimited boards on the dashboard, this will cause performance issues we wish to avoid.

We thank you for your patience!

Danielle

posts.author: BrittanyGo (Brittany O)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks! After more digging, I found that if we could just see the My Week view in a calendar form, that would be exactly what we need. Maybe that wouldn’t be as taxing on the platform?

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Danielle-monday.com:

While we wish we could grant unlimited boards on the dashboard, this will cause performance issues we wish to avoid.

When we signed up to Monday.com it never even occurred to us that a board limit would exist. We assumed that all functionality would be account-wide.

Based on my reading of these forums, most people here seem to work with dozens of active boards, often constantly changing.

So I hope Monday.com have these orders of magnitudes in mind when reviewing board limits vs system performance? Simply doubling or tripling allowances won’t address the issue as far as I can see.

The other issue with board limits is changing which boards are required to be connected to Dashboards.

We often archive boards and create new ones. Updating the boards linked to dashboard widgets is another layer of admin which it seems we must keep track of and manually update. Can Monday provide a better updating option?

posts.author: Thomas-Omnitas (Thomas Karlsson)
posts.cookedHtml:
There are steps being done towards this.
We (everyone with access to monday.labs) just got the new beta features “Use Dashboard cache” and “New dashboards data structure” both features are proof that we are moving in the right direction. Especially the latter is revolutionary for the Dashboard load times. Instead of loading the board(s) ones per widget the Dashboard now loads the board(s) ones for the entire Dashboard and apply the data to all widgets. This is also what allows for Global Dashobard filtering.

IMO this means that we are close co a much higher (maybe even unlimited) count of boards per dashboard.

I know that the devs are working in parallell with making adding board(s) of similar type (based on same template) easier and without need of configuration.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thomas-Omnitas:

IMO this means that we are close co a much higher (maybe even unlimited) count of boards per dashboard.

Good to hear. Achieving unlimited boards would be a massive step forward.

posts.author: mirkoTN (Mirko Grahnert)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Danielle-monday.com would you be able to provide an update on ETA? The limit on boards per dashboard is becoming a real issue for us - especially because there don’t seem to be any workarounds.

Thanks
Mirko

posts.author: thiramiro (Thiago Ramiro)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 needing this over here. This limitation also aplies to custom made monday apps?

posts.author: juliette (Juliette)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Danielle-monday.com,

We are also very interested to know and hear more about the feature to have a higher (maybe unlimited) count of boards per dashboard, especially for the work load widget.

Look forward to hearing from you soon.

Kind regards,
Juliette

posts.author: kylehill (Kyle Hill)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Danielle-monday.com any updates on this feature?

We are transitioning from Asana to Monday.com and this is a critical tool for us. Seeing all of our boards in one place with estimated hours (either in workload widget or ‘my week’ view) is huge.

Thanks!!

posts.author: juliette (Juliette)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Julia-monday.com & @Danielle-monday.com,

I am slightly surprised I haven’t heard anything from anyone from the Monday team.

Look forward to hearing from you shortly.

Kind regards,
Juliette

posts.author: federico.riboldi (federico riboldi)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Julia-monday.com @Danielle-monday.com

Hi, we are new to Monday and really disappointed that there is this limitation on dashboards.

I hope you can give us an answer soon. it’s really annoyng situation. Nobody has tell us about this limitation during the purchase meetings.

Regards,

posts.author: jelitots (Jo Elliott)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @Thomas-Omnitas @Danielle-monday.com @Julia-monday.com Do you have any updates on this matter please? Our small company requires more than 10 boards to be included in our widgets asap. This is currently limiting our reporting capabilities, which is why we chose Monday.com in the first place. Many thanks.

posts.author: Thomas-Omnitas (Thomas Karlsson)
posts.cookedHtml: I know it probably isn’t the answer you were looking for. But if you need more than 10 boards per Dashboard/ widget, you will have to upgrade to Enterprise where you will have capacity up to 50 boards per Dashboard.

posts.author: robertogentile (Roberto Gentile - Inova e-Business)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @Thomas-Omnitas and @jelitots if you open a ticket with Monday Sale’s Team, telling about your necessity, it’s possible that they grows your quantity of boards per widget. In my company, we have 20 boards. I almost left the Monday because of this limitation, when they give to me this possibility, I stayed and stay up to now, satisfied.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
robertogentile:

In my company, we have 20 boards. I almost left the Monday because of this limitation, when they give to me this possibility,

If that’s true, that’s poor business by Monday.com. Should be available to everyone.

We’re trialling other solutions because of this crippling limitation too.

posts.author: robertogentile (Roberto Gentile - Inova e-Business)
posts.cookedHtml: you’re correct @JohnW but I understand that the reason of this is for understand whats the customers need. If a lot of customers need the same thing, the business plan default might be change.

posts.author: jelitots (Jo Elliott)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks so much for the tip!

posts.author: r.bausch (Raphael)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yeah, it’s really hard for us also, because it does not justify our company to upgrade to Enterprise, and we already have to buy a bunch of extra seats that we don’t use because there’s this weird grouping system for pricing that only makes sense if you’re really small o really bit, for the average company, these jumps of 10 then 35 seats are A LOT. Then imagine having to double what you pay per seat, specially seats you don’t use, just because you want to add more than 20 boards do a dashboard.

I mean, offer it as an addon of 1 dollar per user to add more boards or something like that, but this knife to throat approach makes us feels almost like jumping to cheaper competition that doesn’t have these weird arbitrary limits.

posts.author: ckirlin (clare)
posts.cookedHtml: Just hit this roadblock as well. Very difficult to get an aggregate view of all activity within a team when dashboards a limited to 5 boards and we do not need an enterprise account. @Julia-monday.com can you message me the information mentioned above as well? Thanks.

posts.author: ChrisVU (Chris Russell)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @Julia-monday.com and @Danielle-monday.com. I would like to know if there are any updates on this issue. I recently joined a growing company that had been underutilizing and even considered abandoning Monday.com. I believe this platform can be an integral part of our growth, and have been actively advocating expanding our use there of. This issue however, has become the single sticking point that is keeping us from cementing full adoption at a higher level within the company. We are on the brink of upgrading to Enterprise edition which would otherwise be a worthwhile investment for us, but the 50 board limit is a deal breaker. Obviously we would love to see unlimited boards included at any level, but even if we had to pay a premium for the Project Portfolio Management functionality that an increased board limit would offer, we would be extremely interested. I believe that is what many of us here are really looking for: the ability to continue using Monday.com as we grow to a scale that necessitates not only Project Management, but Project Portfolio Management. I for one would love to see Monday.com expand into that arena.

posts.author: sl1nna (Sami Linnanvuo)
posts.cookedHtml:
While we wait monday to increase their dashboard limits, I just wanted to mention that we have developed an add-on, which allows aggregating data from any number of boards into charts that can be placed on monday dashboards. You can also perform calculations between columns on different boards using the Calculated column feature. You can learn more from this blog post.

1750×280 52 KB

The Reports by Screenful won the Staff Pick prize at the Monday Apps Challenge and is now available on the monday marketplace.

posts.author: robertogentile (Roberto Gentile - Inova e-Business)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Thomas-Omnitas Hello!

I leave your live about few minutes ago. I understood, after your CEO not show my question to you, that you don’t work with Time Tracking Column insted of, you use an Interger Column called Hour. I understood that you solution is very cool for many people, but for me, essencially, I need to work with Time Tracking Column.

Correct me if I wrong.

Thanks,

posts.author: Thomas-Omnitas (Thomas Karlsson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Roberto!

Im sorry for the late reply. Your email seems to have fallen between the cracks.
The app we have developed does not work with the time tracking column. We are targeting a different niece.

Hope you found a solution already to your problem!

Cheers

posts.author: ajb (Aaron Becker)
posts.cookedHtml: @Julia-monday.com or @Danielle-monday.com do you have any updates on this functionality. We are currently doing a pilot test of Monday and planning rolling out the enterprise plan company wide however this is such a huge hole in the functionality we cannot move forward without some type of solution. Department level and company wide level visibility into tasks and workload is the primary reason to use a tool like Monday. Any update you can provide would be great. Thank you

posts.author: bradley (Bradley Vanderhorst)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @ajb! At this time we do have limitation on the amount of boards you can have on a dashboard in order to ensure optimal performance.

Have you connected with one of our product consultants or support team to better understand how you intend to manage your data?

Make sure to email support@monday.com, to see how we can best advise on this.

posts.author: ajb (Aaron Becker)
posts.cookedHtml:
Bradley,

I have not worked with a product consultant in detail on this item but that would be helpful if there was a workaround that I do not know about. Would you be able to have someone reach out to me directly via email at aaron@yourprofitbuilders.com ?

Thank you

posts.author: system (system)
posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.

posts.author: Stela1 (Stela)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all,

As in most of the comments I read the need to connect multiple boards in one widget, I wanted to suggest to have a look on the Kanbanify app.

It allows you to connect multiple boards in one single kanban view. You can apply different filters in the Kanban view and get a personalized view that helps you and your team work focused and being more productive.

It works with subitems as well.

Kind Regards
Stela
